title: padus avium var. roseiflora
intro: Padus avium var. roseiflora, also known as the rosebud dogwood or Rosa-canina, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The medicinal properties of Padus avium var. roseiflora include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with this plant include relief from gastrointestinal disorders, improved immune function, and antioxidant properties that may protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Padus avium var. roseiflora include infusions, tinctures, and extracts that can be used to create teas, capsules, and topical creams.
